outputs.purpose: Generated guide to comparison outputs grouped by priority.

Per-eval diff of evals_snapshot.json: added/removed evals plus field-level changes for name / eval_name / stage / tier / difficulty / prompt / expected_output / expectations / assertions / files / expected_refs / expected_scripts. Required reading when provenance_diff shows the causal evals_catalog_semantic_sha256 drifted.
Per-(stage, tier) ws + bs full-pass rates and ws transition counts at old vs new with rollups. Answers 'did stage X improve while tier Y regressed?'
Output family/priority/purpose index for this comparison.
Per-overlap-eval token deltas for ws and bs with pair-completeness flags so incomplete timing is never silently aggregated.
Overlap-only ws/bs full-pass shift, expectation deltas with rubric_sensitive flags, transition tables, and a diagnostic-only McNemar p-value.

Per-eval-intent counts and skill-lift on the overlap. Tracks whether the eval set is balanced for activation behavior — restraint intents (should_not_trigger / near_miss_negative) test whether the skill stays quiet on off-topic prompts; should_trigger tests helpfulness.
4-cell outcome counts at old vs new on the joint set, plus a 4x4 flow matrix with eval_id examples per cell.
Overlap audit: old/new totals, n_overlap, old_only/new_only eval ids. Read this first to confirm what was actually compared.

Skill / src / model / harness / dispatch-template / grader / evals-catalog drift between runs. causal_changed=true is the attribution warning; metadata_changed=true flags label-only differences (round_label / skill_branch / raw snapshot bytes) that do not undermine attribution.
Drill-down for ws regressions and rubric_friction stable_fails: paths to old/new response.md and grading.json so reviewers can open both side-by-side.

Per-regression root-cause classification: rubric / routing / source_selection / tooling / synthesis / unknown. Drives c09 and speeds full-run triage by separating rubric friction from real content regressions.
Bucket counts behind c09: how many regressions fall into each root-cause category.
Per (eval, condition) required-ref / required-script recall and unexpected-resource counts at old vs new. Gated on transcripts on both sides.
Many-to-many (edit_id, eval_id) rows joining each declared edit to its ws/bs transitions and rubric drift flags.
One row per edit_id with transition counts, rubric-changed counts, and all/regressed rubric-friction counts. Renders c04.
One row per overlap eval with ws/bs transitions, rubric drift, token deltas, and regression_interpretation (rubric_friction / rubric_drift / content_regression).
Paired ws/bs full-pass bars at old vs new with overlap-n callout.
Per-overlap-eval ws transition strip, hatched on ws_rubric_changed.
Sankey-lite flow from old outcome buckets to new outcome buckets.
Per-edit_id outcome counts on overlap evals; hatched red marks rubric_friction.
ws token delta per overlap eval, split by ws_transition. Excluded buckets are labeled in a footer rather than rendered as 'no evals'.
Two stacked bar panels: ws required-ref recall delta and required-script recall delta.
Heatmap of ws full-pass rate delta by stage x tier; n/a cells indicate no overlap evals with both ws cells present.
Skill-lift (ws_pass_rate - bs_pass_rate) at old vs new with the delta and 95% bootstrap CIs. Headline answer to 'did the skill help differently between commits?'
Distribution of regression root causes (rubric / routing / source_selection / tooling / synthesis / unknown).
Two-panel view of eval-set balance and per-intent skill-lift; restraint intents are hatched so a positive lift does not read as a win without inspection.
